comments.body:
Hi [~billie.rinaldi]

I like this idea very much, I'd like to leverage this to start CSI adaptor and drivers (YARN-8811). That would help to make the deployment much much more easier. Some comments after reading the design doc
 # Does this allow us to set different configs for AUX services on different nodes? 
 # What happens if a AUX service fail to reload (during upgrade)? Would that crash NM process?
 # I guess this is not depending on HDFS, any implementation of FileSystem would work?
 # I think it needs to expose some query APIs via NMAdmin to check AUX service status, general runtime info, e.g resource usage, port listening, version etc. Does this make sense?

Thanks

Thanks for taking a look, [~cheersyang]!

bq. 1. Does this allow us to set different configs for AUX services on different nodes?
I have thought it would be useful to start different aux services on different nodes. We could have a Service object specify which nodes to run on through its PlacementPolicy (possibly a reduced capability placement policy). This will allow us to have a single manifest, so each NM can read the manifest and determine which aux services it should load. For the first patch, I was only planning to support the same aux services on all nodes, as we do today, but I agree it would be useful to add support for different services on different nodes in a later patch. I may need to add sub-tasks to this ticket.

bq. 2. What happens if a AUX service fail to reload (during upgrade)? Would that crash NM process?
I think aux service init or start failures currently cause runtime exceptions in the NM. 

bq. 3. I guess this is not depending on HDFS, any implementation of FileSystem would work?
Yes, we should be able to read the manifest from any FileSystem.

bq. 4. I think it needs to expose some query APIs via NMAdmin to check AUX service status, general runtime info, e.g resource usage, port listening, version etc. Does this make sense?
I have considered adding an NM REST endpoint such as /ws/v1/node/auxiliaryservices where the user could retrieve information about the aux services that are currently running. I hadn't thought about adding information beyond aux service name and version. Start time sounds like a good candidate for inclusion. I'm not sure how we would get the resource usage and port information when the aux services are running in-process.

[~billie.rinaldi] Patch 004 code works as designed.  Can we can clean up the unused import, JavadocPackage, and VisibilityModifier?  Thanks

If manifest file is changed, node manager does not reload auxiliary service.  Is there a follow up JIRA to make removal of auxiliary service on demand?

Thanks for the review, [~eyang]. I uploaded patch 5 that fixes the checkstyle errors. With the current patch, the NM should detect manifest file changes in 2 minutes. (The NM only considers service name and version when determining if its current version is up to date, so if there are only changes to the service configuration it will not reload the service.) If a service is removed from the manifest, the NM will stop and remove the running aux service. I think we can use YARN-9146 to track improvements in telling the NM when to reload services.

comments.body:
Hi [~billie.rinaldi]

I like this idea very much, I'd like to leverage this to start CSI adaptor and drivers (YARN-8811). That would help to make the deployment much much more easier. Some comments after reading the design doc
 # Does this allow us to set different configs for AUX services on different nodes? 
 # What happens if a AUX service fail to reload (during upgrade)? Would that crash NM process?
 # I guess this is not depending on HDFS, any implementation of FileSystem would work?
 # I think it needs to expose some query APIs via NMAdmin to check AUX service status, general runtime info, e.g resource usage, port listening, version etc. Does this make sense?

Thanks

Thanks for taking a look, [~cheersyang]!

bq. 1. Does this allow us to set different configs for AUX services on different nodes?
I have thought it would be useful to start different aux services on different nodes. We could have a Service object specify which nodes to run on through its PlacementPolicy (possibly a reduced capability placement policy). This will allow us to have a single manifest, so each NM can read the manifest and determine which aux services it should load. For the first patch, I was only planning to support the same aux services on all nodes, as we do today, but I agree it would be useful to add support for different services on different nodes in a later patch. I may need to add sub-tasks to this ticket.

bq. 2. What happens if a AUX service fail to reload (during upgrade)? Would that crash NM process?
I think aux service init or start failures currently cause runtime exceptions in the NM. 

bq. 3. I guess this is not depending on HDFS, any implementation of FileSystem would work?
Yes, we should be able to read the manifest from any FileSystem.

bq. 4. I think it needs to expose some query APIs via NMAdmin to check AUX service status, general runtime info, e.g resource usage, port listening, version etc. Does this make sense?
I have considered adding an NM REST endpoint such as /ws/v1/node/auxiliaryservices where the user could retrieve information about the aux services that are currently running. I hadn't thought about adding information beyond aux service name and version. Start time sounds like a good candidate for inclusion. I'm not sure how we would get the resource usage and port information when the aux services are running in-process.

If manifest file is changed, node manager does not reload auxiliary service.  Is there a follow up JIRA to make removal of auxiliary service on demand?

Thanks for the review, [~eyang]. I uploaded patch 5 that fixes the checkstyle errors. With the current patch, the NM should detect manifest file changes in 2 minutes. (The NM only considers service name and version when determining if its current version is up to date, so if there are only changes to the service configuration it will not reload the service.) If a service is removed from the manifest, the NM will stop and remove the running aux service. I think we can use YARN-9146 to track improvements in telling the NM when to reload services.
